evidence: Scale AI across our business... expanding automation, data driven decision making, and enterprise AI solutions that strengthen productivity and accelerate innovation
additional productivity opportunities to further reduce costs primarily in SI&A, driven in large part by enhanced digital enablement, including automation and AI
scaling AI across R&D, manufacturing, commercial and patient engagement to improve productivity and accelerate innovation
We have begun deploying AI in various parts of our internal and external operations, including in R&D, and continue to explore further use cases for AI
scaling AI across R&D, manufacturing, commercial and patient engagement to improve productivity and accelerate innovation
enhanced digital enablement, including automation and AI, and simplification of business processes

reliance on a small number of AI models or service providers... flawed or inappropriate data practices by data scientists, engineers, and end-users could impair results
risks and challenges related to the use of software, systems and services that include AI-based functionality... cyber-attacks using adversarial AI techniques

capex_mentions.quote: costs associated with all the components of this program to total approximately $5.3 billion
capex_mentions.context: Multi-year cost realignment program including digital enablement and AI, through 2027; not purely AI capex

capex_mentions.quote: approximately $4.7 billion, representing primarily cash expenditures for severance, implementation, exit, and digital enablement costs
capex_mentions.context: Realigning Our Cost Base Program costs through 2027, includes digital enablement/AI component but not AI-exclusive

capex_mentions.quote: approximately $600 million, primarily representing cash expenditures for severance, digital enablement and implementation
capex_mentions.context: R&D productivity program through 2026, includes digital enablement/AI component but not AI-exclusive

limitations: AI investment intensity scored 3 rather than 4/5: the dollar figures cited ($5.3B, $4.7B, $600M) are for broad cost-realignment/digital-enablement programs that include AI but are not AI-specific; no AI-only capex figure is disclosed.
Narrative concreteness scored 3: AI deployment in R&D, manufacturing, commercial, and patient engagement is named but no quantified business results or detailed operational metrics are provided.
The $5.3B and sub-program figures are restructuring/productivity program costs, not pure AI infrastructure capex; included in capex_mentions for completeness with context notes.
Some AI use-case language is aspirational ('explore further use cases'), which tempers concreteness confidence.
